rationale: Severe mismatch: the dark crime-scene aesthetic (#1A1A2E/gold/brown, night, forensic tone) directly contradicts Roof Rails' bright pastel cyan minimalist look, and there is no plausible bridge from "police tape and forensics" into a pole-balancing rooftop runner — any transition would feel like a bait-and-switch and risk store policy flags. Audience skews toward narrative/mystery/casual-puzzle viewers rather than pick-up-and-play hypercasual runners, and the cluster is tiny (1 creative, 1 advertiser) so there is no validated demand signal. Recommend skip; do not greenlight a Roof Rails adaptation of this archetype.
